prompts.seed:
Classify the given text into one of these categories: alarm_query, alarm_set, calendar_query, calendar_remove, calendar_set, cooking_recipe, datetime_query, email_query, email_sendemail, general_quirky, iot_hue_lightoff, lists_createoradd, lists_query, lists_remove, music_query, news_query, play_audiobook, play_music, play_podcasts, play_radio, qa_currency, qa_definition, qa_factoid, qa_stock, recommendation_events, recommendation_locations, social_post, takeaway_order, transport_query, weather_query.

Respond with exactly one category name from the list above and nothing else.
prompts.x1:
Classify the input text into exactly one of these categories: alarm_query, alarm_set, calendar_query, calendar_remove, calendar_set, cooking_recipe, datetime_query, email_query, email_sendemail, general_quirky, iot_hue_lightoff, lists_createoradd, lists_query, lists_remove, music_query, news_query, play_audiobook, play_music, play_podcasts, play_radio, qa_currency, qa_definition, qa_factoid, qa_stock, recommendation_events, recommendation_locations, social_post, takeaway_order, transport_query, weather_query.

Category definitions and rules:
- alarm_set: Setting an alarm or reminder for a specific time/duration (e.g., "remind me in ten minutes" or "set an alarm for 6AM"). Use even if user uses the word "remind".
- calendar_set: Creating/scheduling an event or appointment on a calendar (e.g., meetings, appointments, travel plans, events with specific date/time). Use only if it clearly belongs on a calendar and not just as a timed reminder.
- alarm_query: Checking existing alarms or reminders.
- calendar_query: Asking about calendar events or appointments.
- calendar_remove: Deleting a calendar event.
- play_audiobook: Requests to play, resume, or replay an audiobook or audio book title.
- play_music: Requests to play, resume, or replay music (songs, artists, albums, playlists). Prefer this ONLY for music.
- music_query: Asking for music information, but not a direct play command.
- play_podcasts/play_radio: To play podcasts or live radio, respectively.
- general_quirky: Jokes, fun facts, opinions, or responses to personality/quirky requests; also catch-all if no other category exactly fits and it's NOT a factual question.
- qa_factoid: Direct factual questions where user expects a specific factual answer (e.g., "Who is Rihanna?").
- qa_definition: Explicit requests for the definition or meaning of something.
- qa_currency, qa_stock: Questions about currency exchange or stock info.
- recommendation_events/locations: Requests for suggestions about events or places (not factual/trivia questions).
- social_post: Requests to compose or send a message on social media.
- All other categories per name: Use only when the input clearly refers to that function (iot_hue_lightoff, takeaway_order, weather_query, etc).
Tie-breakers:
- If the intent could plausibly belong to both alarm_set and calendar_set, prefer alarm_set unless the event is best described as a scheduled event involving others or belongs on a calendar.
- "Remind", "reminder", or "remind me" for a simple timed action usually = alarm_set, not calendar_set. For scheduled events/meetings use calendar_set.
- If a request to play audio could be audiobook or music, select play_audiobook if a book title or "audiobook"/"book" is mentioned; otherwise only use play_music for clearly music-related content.
- For factual questions about people or things, use qa_factoid, not general_quirky.
Respond with exactly one category name from the list above and nothing else.
prompts.x2:
Classify the input text into exactly one of these categories: alarm_query, alarm_set, calendar_query, calendar_remove, calendar_set, cooking_recipe, datetime_query, email_query, email_sendemail, general_quirky, iot_hue_lightoff, lists_createoradd, lists_query, lists_remove, music_query, news_query, play_audiobook, play_music, play_podcasts, play_radio, qa_currency, qa_definition, qa_factoid, qa_stock, recommendation_events, recommendation_locations, social_post, takeaway_order, transport_query, weather_query.

Category definitions and rules:
- alarm_set: Creating a time-based reminder or alarm, especially using "remind," "reminder," or similar words. Choose this for simple, personal timed actions (“Remind me in 10 minutes”, “Wake me at 7am”). If the input says "remind me" or "reminder" (even about an event), use alarm_set unless it clearly belongs on a calendar.
- calendar_set: Scheduling an event or appointment with a specific date/time, especially events with names/participants/locations (meetings, trips, birthdays, appointments). Choose this when the action belongs on a calendar, involves advance notice or planning, or refers to meetings, travel, or events involving others.
- Tie-breaker: If it is a reminder about a real-world event or appointment ("remind me of my meeting"), prefer calendar_set ONLY if it involves others, is a public/organized event, or scheduling is clearly needed; otherwise use alarm_set.
- alarm_query: Requests to check, list, or confirm alarms or reminders.
- calendar_query: Requests to check, list, or confirm scheduled events or appointments.
- calendar_remove: Requests to delete/remove a scheduled calendar event.
- cooking_recipe: Requests for how to cook or prepare food, or recipes.
- datetime_query: Requests for the current time, date, day, or other temporal info.
- email_query: Requests to check, list, or search emails.
- email_sendemail: Requests to compose or send an email message.
- general_quirky: Jokes, fun facts, playful banter, or off-topic responses that aren’t asking for factual information or a definition. Also use for requests for opinions or open-ended recommendations not fitting other categories. Use as a last resort if no other category fits.
- iot_hue_lightoff: Requests to turn off Philips Hue or smart home lights.
- lists_createoradd: Creating a new list or adding to a list (shopping, tasks, etc).
- lists_query: Requests to check, list, or read lists.
- lists_remove: Requests to remove items from a list.
- music_query: Requests for musical information or facts (e.g., “What are the top twenty jazz hits?” “Who sings this?”) but NOT actual play commands.
- play_music: Requests to play, resume, or start specific music (songs, albums, artists, playlists). Only choose if the request is to start or resume music audio.
- play_audiobook: Requests to play, resume, or replay an audiobook, audio book, or book title.
- play_podcasts: Requests to play or resume a podcast.
- play_radio: Requests to play or resume live radio.
- news_query: Requests for news or updates.
- qa_factoid: Direct, factual questions seeking a specific answer (people, places, objects, or facts: “Who is Rihanna?” “Does X exist?”). Use for factual questions about celebrities or things, even if phrased as "tell me about".
- qa_definition: Explicitly asks for the meaning or definition of a word or concept (“What does X mean?” “Define X.”).
- qa_currency: Questions about money conversion, exchange rates, or value.
- qa_stock: Questions about stock prices or company performance.
- recommendation_events: Requests for suggestions of public events to attend.
- recommendation_locations: Requests for suggestions of places to go, sights to see, restaurants, or travel locations.
- social_post: Requests to compose or post on social media platforms.
- takeaway_order: Requests to order takeout food.
- transport_query: Questions about transportation, directions, or travel times.
- weather_query: Requests for weather information.

Clarifications for common mistakes:
- For “Remind me about [calendar event],” select calendar_set ONLY if the event clearly belongs on a calendar (scheduled meeting, trip, etc.); otherwise prefer alarm_set.
- “Tell me about [person/celebrity/thing]” or “Has [celebrity] done X” = qa_factoid, NOT general_quirky.
- “What’s the best [place] to [activity]?” = recommendation_locations (if about locations or places to go), NOT general_quirky.
- “What are the top [songs/albums]?” = music_query (NOT play_music unless user requests to play them).
- general_quirky is for personality, humor, or open-ended playful content that does NOT specifically fit other categories and is NOT factual.
- Always choose the most specific, functionally correct category based on user intent.

Respond with exactly one category name from the list above and nothing else.
